query: Boeing MQ-28 Ghost Bat advanced capabilities extended combat reach unveiling details 2026

{"query":"Boeing MQ-28 Ghost Bat advanced capabilities extended combat reach unveiling details 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1598,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://boeing.mediaroom.com/news-releases-statements?item=131675","title":"News Releases | Boeing Newsroom","content":"Boeing unveils advanced MQ-28 capabilities, extended combat reach
­- More than 25% percent larger wing expands mission range and boosts stores capacity
BERLIN, GERMANY June 10, 2026 – Boeing [NYSE: BA] unveiled extended combat capabilities of the MQ-28 Ghost Bat at the ILA Berlin Air Show in Germany.
Other new MQ-28 capabilities include:
Introduction of Beyond Line of Sight (BLOS) communication links – enabling the MQ-28 custodian to operate from a crewed aircraft, ground station or Naval vessel at unlimited standoff distances.
A leading global aerospace company and top U.S. exporter, Boeing develops, manufactures and services commercial airplanes, defense products and space systems for customers in more than 150 countries."},{"url":"https://theaviationist.com/2026/06/10/boeing-unveils-new-capabilities-for-mq-28-ghost-bat/","title":"Boeing Unveils New Capabilities for MQ-28 Ghost Bat - The Aviationist","content":"Boeing unveiled at ILA Berlin 2026 the new capabilities within the MQ-28 Ghost Bat’s roadmap, including internal weapon bays and larger wings.
The announced enhancements include 25% larger wings, which allows the MQ-28 to carry an additional 2,000 pounds of fuel, stores, and mission payloads.
The increased wingspan allows the drone to reach a Maximum Take Off Weight (MTOW) of 12,000 lb (5,400 kg), with a useful load of 4,500 lb (2,000 kg).
However, the new capabilities appear to be in line with some of the details already released on the Block 3.
“The advanced maturity of the MQ-28 systems is what allows us to continually adapt to the changing operational environment and minimise the risk as we transition to operations,” said Amy List, vice president and managing director of Boeing Defence Australia, at ILA 2026.
On that occasion, the aircraft teamed up with an E-7 Wedgetail and an F/A-18F Super Hornet of the Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF).
In May 2026, Boeing disclosed that the MQ-28 Ghost Bat is now flying in the U.S., in what has been defined as the first flights abroad.
It is possible, however, that two aircraft are currently there as two different aircraft can be seen in the December 2025 and May 2026 videos."},{"url":"https://boeing.mediaroom.com/news-releases-statements?item=131673","title":"News Releases | Boeing Newsroom","content":"BRISBANE, Queensland, June 1, 2026 — Boeing [NYSE:BA] has validated another advanced capability for the MQ-28 collaborative combat aircraft, which gives customers a decisive edge in combat: stealth performance."},{"url":"https://aerospaceglobalnews.com/news/mq28-ghost-bat-live-fire-milestone-australia-production-order/","title":"Boeing MQ-28 Ghost Bat marks live-fire milestone with AIM-120","content":"The demonstration, part of Trial Kareela 25-4 at RAAF Base Woomera on 8 December, saw the uncrewed aircraft collaborate with an E-7A Wedgetail and an F/A-18F Super Hornet in a realistic counter-air mission.
“The Ghost Bat turns a single fighter into a combat team,” Conroy said.

Elevation, or pitch, is one angle engineers analyze to validate MQ-28’s radar detectability inside Boeing’s test chamber. **BRISBANE, Queensland, June 1, 2026** — Boeing [NYSE:BA] has validated another advanced capability for the MQ-28 collaborative combat aircraft, which gives customers a decisive edge in combat: stealth performance. This milestone further demonstrates the platform’s maturity, survivability, and ability to deliver cost-effective advanced capability for modern air combat operations. One way to measure the stealth of an aircraft is Radar Cross Section (RCS) testing. The MQ-28’s RCS reduces the range that enemy radars can detect and engage an MQ-28, enhancing the platform’s survivability in contested environments. The success of the RCS testing confirms the effectiveness of the MQ-28’s design, production and material choices in minimising radar detection. * Demonstrating interoperability by teaming two in-flight MQ-28s plus one digital aircraft with an airborne E-7A Wedgetail to complete a mission against an airborne target.","score":0.8156003,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://boeing.mediaroom.com/news-releases-statements?item=131682","title":"News Releases | Boeing Newsroom","content":"During Valiant Shield 2026 in June, the Australian-developed MQ-28 Ghost Bat integrated with forces from the U.S. Pacific Command to support the USAF's","score":0.8025714,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.86,"request_id":"9a8a3e91-e740-4aff-aa50-9bcd5b312f99"}
